revenue_recognition.description: Revenue recognized per ASC 606. Management and franchise fees recognized over time as services performed. Owned/leased revenues recognized daily as rooms occupied. Loyalty program revenues based on points redeemed including breakage estimates. Third-party actuaries assist with breakage calculations.

depreciation_amortization.description: D&A was $333M (2024), $397M (2023), $426M (2022). Declining trend reflects asset-light strategy with property dispositions.

goodwill.description: Goodwill of $2,541M (2024). Evaluated annually during Q4 using DCF models, guideline public company methods, and third-party appraisals. $110M impairment charge in 2024 on a management and franchising reporting unit.

lease_accounting.description: Operating lease ROU assets: $328M (2024). Operating lease liabilities: $278M current + long-term. Weighted-average remaining term: 14 years (operating), 2 years (finance). Weighted-average discount rate: 3.8% (operating), 2.0% (finance). Total minimum operating lease payments: $353M.

loyalty_program.description: World of Hyatt deferred revenue liability: $1,333M (2024). Actuarially determined based on anticipated timing of future point redemptions including breakage estimate. Critical audit matter — breakage assumption is subjective.

income_taxes.description: Provision for income taxes: $267M (2024), $90M (2023), benefit of $92M (2022). Deferred tax assets: $466M (2024). Includes deferred income tax benefit of $123M from timing differences.

stock_compensation.description: Share-based payment activity: $32M (2024). SARs outstanding: 3.24M shares at weighted avg exercise price $76.59. RSUs nonvested: 873K shares. SAR intrinsic value outstanding: $261M.

subsequent_events.description: Form 8-K filed March 26, 2025 (Exhibit A-2). Contains forward-looking statements about company strategy. No material subsequent events identified in the audited financial statements.

related_party_transactions.description: Hyatt Corporation provides Mandatory and Non-Mandatory Services to franchised Brand Hotels. Rosemont provides optional purchasing services. System Services Costs are at actual cost without markup on Mandatory Services, but may include profit on Non-Mandatory Services. Franchise agreement assets ($144.8M on franchisor balance sheet) represent payments to customers — likely key money and franchise incentives.

advertising_fund_accounting.description: No separate advertising fund. Marketing covered by 3.5% Commercial Services Fee. No periodic accounting provided. Franchisor need not spend any amount in franchisee's market area. World of Hyatt loyalty funds deposited in segregated accounts with independent investment managers; independently audited annually.
